$schema_comment: Shared fixture for the managed-domain coverage predicate (Step O spec §3.2, RFC 6125 §6.4.3). Consumed by Go test TestIsHostCoveredByManagedDomain_FixtureFile + the future TS port test (web/frontend/src/routes/routes/+page.svelte::findCoveringManagedDomain), when a vitest harness lands per docs/backlog-step-o.md #O.4-1. The fixture is the SINGLE source of truth: drift between the two implementations fails one of the two tests in CI.
$rules: host is covered by managed domain `apex` iff host has exactly ONE more label than the apex (single-label wildcard rule, RFC 6125 §6.4.3)
multi-label depth (e.g. `deep.app.example.com` vs apex `example.com`) is NOT covered
bare apex is covered iff IncludeApex == true (spec D2.C)
host and apex are both lowercase+trailing-dot-stripped before compare (DNS is case-insensitive; trailing dot is canonical per RFC 1035)
wildcard route-host (`*.foo`) is NEVER covered — we EMIT the wildcard, we don't consume it
empty managed-domains slice short-circuits to not-covered for every host (spec D5.A byte-equality invariant)
managed-domain rows with empty apex are skipped defensively

cases.name: bare apex NOT covered when includeApex=false
single-label sub of includeApex=false IS covered (wildcard still applies)
host with no managed-domain suffix NOT covered
wildcard route-host itself NOT covered (we emit it, not consume it)
D6.A multi — single-label sub of alpha.com resolves to alpha
D6.A multi — single-label sub of beta.com resolves to beta
D6.A multi — host unrelated to any apex NOT covered
label that is just digits covered
host equal to wildcard form NOT covered (defensive symmetry)
